mechanism_narrative: A sustained high level of the federal funds rate indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance, which increases the cost of capital for businesses and consumers, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS value of 4.76% as of t-1mo is historically high
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate an economic downturn.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE value of 4.7% as of t-1mo is still low but any rise is concerning
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the signal's predictive power (Rank IC) is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in both retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based weakness in consumer demand and investment, suggesting a significant economic slowdown that negatively impacts corporate revenues and S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST has been declining from peak levels
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in > 40% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST are declining, or the interaction term fails to add significant explanatory power beyond individual series.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a deceleration in GDP growth, even if still positive, signals an approaching peak in the business cycle, leading investors to anticipate slower earnings growth and thus negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth rate declines for two consecutive quarters
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 value of 11630.7 as of t-4mo
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances during late-cycle expansions, or the signal's direction flips to positive in such regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth indicates robust manufacturing activity and business investment, translating into higher corporate revenues and profits, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO value of 114.357 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO's 1-month change against 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.01 over any 5-year rolling window, or the correlation turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by CPI, erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, leading to investor concerns about economic stability and potential central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL value of 208.903 as of t-1mo, implying high YoY growth
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the correlation between high inflation and negative returns is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and overall economic activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS value of 138421.0 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS's 1-month change against 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.01 over any 5-year rolling window, or the correlation turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is high while inflation remains elevated, it signals a restrictive monetary policy struggling to contain inflation, creating an environment of high discount rates and uncertain economic outlook, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year percentage change > 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS value of 4.76% as of t-1mo
fact:CPIAUCSL value of 208.903 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the combined effect of high FEDFUNDS and high CPIAUCSL does not significantly predict negative returns.
